question_text: Which of these is NOT an example of static electricity?

options.label: B
options.text: Getting a shock from a metal door handle

options.label: C
options.text: Current flowing through a light bulb

options.label: D
options.text: A balloon sticking to a wall after being rubbed

explanation: Current flowing through a light bulb is an example of moving electric current (dynamic electricity), not static electricity. Static electricity involves charges that are stationary and build up on objects.
